🇺🇸**US hunt for Iranian oil spills beyond Hormuz into Asian waters**

The US Navy seized several tankers that were ostensibly carrying Iranian crude in the Indian Ocean this week:

➡️ Tifani, boarded on April 21

➡️  Deep Sea, Sevin and Dorena, were reported as intercepted by the US Navy on April 22

➡️  Majestic X, boarded on April 23

🔴 All of these vessels were seized by US warships far from the Strait of Hormuz, which means that the US campaign against shipping with Iranian oil extends beyond the Gulf region.

🔴 The US may pressure littoral states in Southeast Asia to police their waters in order to crack down on such tankers, the South China Morning Post suggests.

🔴 Such seizures may raise legal questions under the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, especially if interdictions move into regional waters.

📌 In the meantime, US seizures of the tankers contribute to:

🔺 Oil prices increases

🔺 Rising costs of shipping insurance

🔺 Greater volatility in freight markets

🔺 Further delays in Persian Gulf transit

What Washington calls enforcement, many others may see as selective piracy with a flag attached.

❔ **What if 5G and Covid vaccine conspiracy theories were right all along?**

US Patent US10786570B2, assigned to Rockefeller University and titled “Ferritin Nanoparticle Compositions and Methods to Modulate Cell Activity,” details methods for remotely-controlling living cells.

This result is achieved by targeting nanoparticles previously introduced into cells, which can alter their behavior and either prompt them to produce certain proteins or stop doing so, like an on/off switch.

Sounds like a benign invention that can help treat various diseases – provided that the control signal is in the right hands.

full_text:
👁 **Meta is watching your every click to train AI to replace you
**
Meta has begun installing new tracking software on US-employee computers, capturing mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes to feed its artificial intelligence models. The goal is to build autonomous AI agents that can perform work tasks without human intervention.

Here is what you need to know:

🌏 The tool, dubbed Model Capability Initiative (MCI), doesn’t just track activity on work-related apps and websites. It also takes occasional screenshots of employees’ screens, according to internal memos reviewed by staff. The purpose is to teach AI where it still fumbles, like navigating dropdown menus or using keyboard shortcuts.

🌏 “This is where all Meta employees can help our models get better simply by doing their daily work,” one memo read. Translation: Your routine labor is now training data.

🌏 Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth announced an internal rebranding of these efforts as the Agent Transformation Accelerator (ATA). His vision is stark:  

💬 “One where our agents primarily do the work and our role is to direct, review, and help them improve.”

🌏 Agents, he said, would learn to “automatically see where we felt the need to intervene so they can be better next time.” He stopped short of spelling out how, but Meta spokesperson Andy Stone confirmed: MCI data will be part of the recipe.

🌏 Meta insists the data won’t be used for performance assessments. Safeguards exist for “sensitive content,” though the company won’t say what’s excluded. Stone explained: “If we’re building agents to help people complete everyday tasks… our models need real examples of how people actually use [computers].” In other words, show the machine how you work, so it no longer needs you.

🌏 Meta is already planning to cut 10% of its global workforce starting May 20, with more layoffs expected. Amazon trimmed 30,000 corporate roles. Block cut nearly half its staff. Across Silicon Valley, AI is no longer a tool. It’s a headcount reduction strategy.

🌏 Internally, Meta has urged employees to use AI agents even if it slows them down in the short term. Job distinctions are being erased in favor of a new generalist title: “AI builder.” A new engineering team, Applied AI (AAI), is already absorbing the company’s “strong” software engineers to build models that can eventually do the bulk of coding, testing, and shipping.

👍** **[**Boost us**](https://t.me/boost/geopolitics_prime)** | **[**Chat**](https://t.me/+tltgWD-GUqw0OTAy)** | ****@geopolitics_prime**
headline: 👁 **Meta is watching your every click to train AI to replace you
